setting.note: A quietly dystopian England where children raised at a secluded boarding school are clones bred to give up their organs. The map runs from the rural southern countryside that hides Hailsham, north-east through the remote farmland of the Cottages to the Norfolk seaside at Cromer, and out along the flat coastal fields Kathy drives as a carer.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Kazuo Ishiguro, first published 2005. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Never Let Me Go, Kazuo Ishiguro (2005). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The cold grey-blue of the North Sea off Norfolk and the overcast English light the book lives under. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
